Container.Name: peaks
Container.Overview: Opus/peaks — learns your taste from example stills, finds the matching moments across a Stash library, writes them back as scene markers, and plays them on a continuously-cycling "megaboard" grid (port 8800). All other commands (embed, score, label, train) run from this container's console. Requires the Nvidia Driver plugin + --runtime=nvidia for GPU embedding. IMPORTANT: the Media path below must be mounted at the SAME container path your Stash container uses, so file paths reported by Stash resolve here.

Config.Name: Labeler
Config.Description: The Tier-2 frame-rating page (only live while 'peaks label' runs).

Config.Name: Config / data
Config.Description: Settings, embedding cache, models, labels, reference stills.

Config.Name: Media (match Stash's container path!)
Config.Description: Your video library. CRITICAL: set the host path to the same share Stash uses, and edit the container path ('/data') to whatever path your STASH container mounts it at — check your Stash container's settings. Read-only is fine.

Config.Name: STASH_URL
Config.Description: Base URL of your Stash server.

Config.Name: STASH_API_KEY
Config.Description: Stash > Settings > Security > API Key. Leave empty if Stash has no auth.

Config.Name: PEAKS_DEVICE
Config.Description: cuda = use the GPU for embedding; cpu = no GPU.

Config.Name: PEAKS_HWACCEL
Config.Description: GPU-assisted video DECODE (NVDEC). 'cuda' is a big speedup on H.264/HEVC; set to empty if some files fail to decode, or 'auto' to let ffmpeg choose.

Config.Name: PEAKS_LIBRARY_PATH
Config.Description: Only look at files whose path starts with this (blank = whole library). Use it to exclude VR etc. — e.g. /data/Rando. Must be the CONTAINER path (matches Stash's), including subfolders.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
Config.Description: Which GPU(s) the container may use ('all', or a GPU UUID from Settings > Nvidia Driver).

Config.Name: NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES
Config.Description: Leave as 'all' (compute for embedding + video for NVDEC decode).
